title: HTML Fundamentals
description: Learn structure, semantic tags, and forms.
notes: HTML builds the structure of every webpage using tags like heading, paragraph, and section.
Use semantic tags such as header, main, article, and footer to improve readability and accessibility.
Forms collect user data through input, select, textarea, and button elements.

title: CSS for Responsive Design
description: Build modern layouts with Flexbox and Grid.
notes: CSS styles your HTML and controls layout, spacing, color, and typography.
Flexbox is great for one-dimensional layouts, while Grid is ideal for two-dimensional sections.
Media queries allow your UI to adapt to mobile, tablet, and desktop screens.

title: JavaScript Essentials
description: Understand DOM events, arrays, and objects.
notes: JavaScript adds interactivity by responding to user actions like clicks and keyboard input.
The DOM lets JavaScript read and update HTML elements dynamically.
Arrays and objects help you organize application data and build reusable logic.

title: Mini Project Workshop
description: Combine HTML/CSS/JS into a real mini app.
notes: Start with a clear folder structure and split code by responsibility.
Build one feature at a time and test each page flow before moving to the next feature.
Final polish includes responsive checks, usability improvements, and bug fixes.
